---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

```

## Targeted Advertising Analysis

This report analyses patterns in targeted advertisements observed across different social media platforms. It explores the types of ads shown and how relevant they are to the user.

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Load data
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQR_G_3yEKNwFvx9spVkD_Axk0cMDp05GT7xUyU9-VkBifoj6qEFR6HfgEEMd82MD8YGo3hlIlaUExP/pub?gid=257118034&single=true&output=csv")

# Rename variables
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(platform = "Which platform did you see this ad on?",
    ad_type = "What type of product or content is being advertised?",
    relevance = "How relevant is this ad to you? (1 = Not relevant, 10 = Very relevant)",
    interaction = "Did you interact with the ad?")

# Create summary values
mean_relevance <- mean(latest_data$relevance)
max_relevance <- max(latest_data$relevance)

# Dynamic text
paste("The average relevance score of ads is", round(mean_relevance, 2))
paste("The highest relevance score observed was", max_relevance)

# Bar charts
# Chart 1
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = platform, fill = platform)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Number of Ads by Platform",
       x = "Platform",
       y = "Count") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
# Chart 2
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = ad_type, fill = ad_type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Ad Types Observed",
    x = "Ad Type",
    y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
  legend.position = "none") 

```
